opp.next_step: Confidence 30% 2nd Jul - sent intro email for Jihad and Hakim, to follow up with meeting 10th June - RFP response submitted, some discount retained for future negotiation. 3rd June - Clarifications received - will rebuild the commercials accordingly, there is some complexity around phasing of the business units, but will bundle into one (as we did with SAMI). 25th May - RFP deadline extended to 10th June. 7th May - RFP received, under review - clarifications deadline 15th May, Response deadline 20th May. 30th Mar - Re below - to help him build out budget approval I have built a quick ROI calculator and shared with him a draft output, suggesting we build it out together and co-present to the CFO - may help move it forward and get further up the decision ladder. 25th Mar - Update from CPO... " Thank you for your follow-up and for checking in. Discussions regarding the bid management solution are still underway as we work on securing budget approval , so some activities are on hold for now. I appreciate your patience and will reconnect as soon as we have the green light to move forward." ... moving back to Q2. 13th Mar - Haven't heard anything from CPO since below.... nudged to arrange a catch up after Eid. 23rd Jan - After nudging Jihad the CPO received this update... "I wanted to give you an update on our current progress. We are in the midst of setting our procurement strategy, with a primary focus on the digitalization and automation of our procurement function. At this stage, we have submitted our procurement strategy to the management for their review and approval. Once we receive their approval, we will move forward with the selection process to identify the best procurement solution that aligns with our goals. Let's plan to have our discussion once we have finalized everything internally. I appreciate your understanding and look forward to catching up soon." DQ: Want to try and turn Jihad into a champion - I have offered live demo to the team f2f in Abu Dhabi, also to support the business case and share any helpful case studies & metrics, importantly stressed the need to keep a regular communication cadence ....will look in each time I am in Abu Dhabi to keep the rapport up. Also GMQ 26 communicated. 14th Jan waiting on confirmation of F2F meeting 7th Jan - Pricing submitted to CPO with a request to run through it f2f ASAP. 30th Dec - SaaS received with Hrishi for implementation. 20th Dec - Pricing with Raj for Saas 19th Dec - After several nudges, still no response to the table - spoke to Arun, he said we will have to estimate, can't/won't provide further detail. 20th Nov - Shared a completed/estimated usage table to get pricing - lots of guesses - awaiting confirmation this scenario is realistic. 30th Oct - Met with Jihad (CPO) and Arun onsite in Abu Dhabi, constructive run through the pricing template, but they have a lot of gaps in data for the QIT (including no idea what their revenue or spend is ...not unusual for a ME private co), Arun to come back with some info & DQ to extrapolate into scenarios for a QIT - not as far advanced in the as they had said (can't see how they have shortlisted Ariba and Coupa ... think it's been informal) - vague on timelines est 3-6 months, DQ offered to present to the steering committee and build an ROI/Biz Case if needed.... will keep as end Q4 close and monitor as it progresses. 28th Oct - Confirmed meeting CPO (Jihad) at his office in Abu Dhabi Thurs 30th Oct 22nd Oct - No response yet, suggest a F2F in Abu Dhabi next week 16th Oct - Nudged to arrange the follow up call 8th Oct - Open to reviewing Zycus, but wants pricing, shared the estimation file with him to understand scope, review and arrange a follow up call. 30th Sept - Jihad back from travel - catch up call scheduled for 8th 9th Sept - CPO (Jihad) attended the Abu Dhabi World Tour event - are running a formal evaluation and shortlisted Ariba and Coupa, is interested in adding Zycus, will be travelling, but will reach out to us on his return 22nd Sept... DQ to nudge if no response by 24th.

items.quote: Jihad Ibrahim is the decision maker, but will require senior approval (CFO TBC).
Chad Bautista is logged as Executive Sponsor contact role but has never appeared on a read call.
RFP response submitted 10th June, some discount retained for future negotiation.

metrics.narrative: The stated business drivers are group-wide efficiency and total cost of ownership reduction: on the 8 Oct 2025 call the CPO said the group wants to 'achieve efficiency of our work, reduce the total cost of ownership somehow' via AI-integrated tools, but no dollar figure or cycle-time metric has ever been quantified. Zycus tried to close that gap on 30 Mar 2026 by building a draft ROI calculator to co-present to the CFO, but there is no confirmation the session happened or that a number was agreed. The move to quantify savings remains open.

champion.narrative: Jihad Ibrahim is being actively developed as champion: the deal owner explicitly noted on 23 Jan 2026 wanting 'to turn Jihad into a champion,' offering a live f2f demo in Abu Dhabi and committing to a regular cadence. He is responsive and has requested next steps himself (8 Oct 2025 call), but engagement has repeatedly required nudging (16 Oct, 22 Oct 2025, 13 Mar 2026), and he has no confirmed access to the EB. What has developed: direct working relationship and an RFP submitted on his ask; what is still missing: EB access and consistent unprompted cadence.

competition.narrative: National Holding shortlisted Coupa and SAP Ariba before Zycus was added, per the CPO's own account at the 9 Sep 2025 World Tour event, and both remain live in the same formal RFP submitted 10 Jun 2026. On the 8 Oct 2025 call the buyer directly asked how Zycus compares, and the Zycus rep positioned Zycus 'closer to Coupa' on capability with an AI-maturity wedge (generative AI studio since 2018) versus a more dated Ariba stack. No evaluation feedback has surfaced since to confirm relative standing.

identify_pain.narrative: The CPO articulated fragmented, manual procurement across a multi-entity group as the core pain on the 8 Oct 2025 call, tying it directly to a fiscal year-end budget planning cycle: 'we are at end of the year where we need to check our budget... understand the commercial part of it, and we'll take it from there with our management.' The pain owner is the CPO (Jihad Ibrahim), with the underlying driver being lack of group-level visibility and cost control, not yet quantified.

sources: No evidence in any Avoma call, Next_Step__c, or field history
paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, InfoSec) have been discussed; the deal has not progressed past RFP evaluation. Visibility will only emerge once National Holding confirms a vendor-selection decision and Zycus is invited into a paper stage; until then this remains an open unknown consistent with the deal's Formal Evaluation stage.

economic_buyer.narrative: Jihad Ibrahim (Group Director of Procurement) is confirmed as Decision Maker but explicitly does not hold budget authority; per the CRM MEDDPICC record (updated 15 May 2026) he 'will require senior approval (TBC)'. Salesforce carries two group-level CFO-titled contacts, Fareed Bilbeis (Group Chief Financial Officer) and Saed Arekat (Group CFO), but neither has appeared on any read call or been confirmed by Jihad as the actual budget approver. The CPO's 25 Mar 2026 note that discussions are 'on hold' while 'securing budget approval' confirms the EB has not yet engaged directly.

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, multi-vendor RFP: shortlisted against Coupa and SAP Ariba since 9 Sep 2025, RFP issued 7 May 2026, extended once (clarifications to 15 May, response to 20 May, then final deadline 10 Jun 2026), submitted 10 Jun 2026, and now sits in an evaluation period with no scoring feedback communicated 27 days later. The stated downstream steps are internal management review then a vendor-selection decision, per both the CPO's own words and the CRM MEDDPICC record.

decision_criteria.narrative: On the 8 Oct 2025 call the buyer laid out three explicit criteria: group-wide applicability ('we need to see how this will help our strategic goals... efficiency... reduce total cost of ownership'), implementation approach (in-house vs. Big Four partner), and pricing/licensing structure (module- and user-based). These were reconfirmed operationally when the RFP was issued 7 May 2026 and a clarification round on business-unit phasing was resolved 3 Jun 2026 ('complexity around phasing of the business units, but will bundle into one, as we did with SAMI').

The ball is now in Jihad's court — deal is effectively paused until he returns from leave.

Position AI/Merlin as the group-wide efficiency unlock and the wedge against Ariba/Coupa — National Holding's stated criteria are efficiency gains and TCO reduction across the group; Zycus's positioned differentiator is AI maturity ('7-8 years ahead', generative AI since 2018). But the RFP response (submitted 10 Jun 2026) is a black box — there is no confirmation the AI/Merlin narrative landed or that the evaluation committee understands how Zycus's AI capabilities translate to measurable efficiency vs Ariba/Coupa's newer AI plays. The next move is to offer a post-RFP AI deep-dive session (30-45 min) where Dan walks the evaluation committee through 2-3 live Merlin use cases (AI-driven sourcing event setup, contract risk extraction, spend anomaly detection) and quantifies the time savings vs manual workflows. Use the session to reframe the RFP decision as 'AI-mature platform today (Zycus) vs AI catch-up roadmap (Ariba/Coupa)', and anchor the efficiency gains to the group's procurement digitalization strategy (23 Jan 2026 email from Jihad).

Re-land the AI differentiation story (2018 Generative AI Studio, full S2P suite) directly with Jihad Ibrahim in writing now, since it was delivered live on 8 Oct 2025 but has not been reinforced since RFP submission.

hard.ais_why:
• On 23 Jan 2026, CPO Jihad wrote to the rep: 'We are in the midst of setting our procurement strategy, with a primary focus on the digitalization and automation of our procurement function. At this stage, we have submitted our procurement strategy to the management for their review and approval.' Buyer framing is 'digitalization and automation' ‚Äî adjacent to AI but not naming AI/Merlin/agentic.
• On 7 May 2026: 'RFP received, under review - clarifications deadline 15th May, Response deadline 20th May.' On 30 Mar 2026: 'to help him build out budget approval I have built a quick ROI calculator and shared with him a draft output, suggesting we build it out together and co-present to the CFO.' Jihad is engaging deeply but the conversation is digitalization/ROI focused. ANA is in product scope (only AI SKU); Merlin/Merlin Intake/Agentic AI are absent.
• Confidence is Medium. National Holding has a real RFP in flight (received 7 May), an engaged CPO (Jihad), and a stated digitalization/automation thesis. But 'digitalization and automation' is process-language, not AI-specific. With only ANA in AI scope and no explicit AI demand from buyer, the score sits at 5 (boundary DOWN per rubric).
